title: Compute nCr % m
hints: nCr = n! / ((n-r)! * r!). We need to compute this modulo m.
Directly computing factorials can cause overflow. Use factorization approach.
Factorize each number in numerator (n, n-1, ..., n-r+1) and denominator (1, 2, ..., r).
Subtract the exponents of denominator factors from numerator factors.
Multiply the remaining factors using modular exponentiation to avoid overflow.
Use the property: (a * b) % m = ((a % m) * (b % m)) % m.
For prime factorization, iterate from 2 to sqrt(n) and count occurrences.
